author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XV

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A psalm of David himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Touching this title there is no question.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord who shall sojourn in Thy tabernacle?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Although tabernacle be sometimes used even for an everlasting habitation: yet when tabernacle is taken in its proper meaning, it is a thing of war.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hence soldiers are called tent-fellows, as having their tents together.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This sense is assisted by the words, “Who shall sojourn?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we war with the devil for a time, and then we need a tabernacle wherein we may refresh ourselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which specially points out the faith of the temporal Dispensation, which was wrought for us in time through the Incarnation of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And who shall rest in Thy holy mountain?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here perhaps he signifies at once the eternal habitation itself, that we should understand by “mountain” the supereminence of the love of Christ in life eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He who walketh without stain, and worketh righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here he has laid down the proposition; in what follows he sets it forth in detail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who speaketh the truth in his heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For some have truth on their lips, and not in their heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As if one should deceitfully point out a road, knowing that there were robbers there, and should say, If you go this way, you will be safe from robbers; and it should turn out that in fact there were no robbers found there: he has spoken the truth, but not in his heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he supposed it to be otherwise, and spoke the truth in ignorance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore it is not enough to speak the truth, unless it be so also in heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who hath practised no deceit in his tongue” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deceit is practised with the tongue, when one thing is professed with the mouth, another concealed in the breast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nor done evil to his neighbour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is well known that by “neighbour,” every man should be understood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And hath not entertained slander against his neighbour,” that is, hath not readily or rashly given credence to an accuser.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The malicious one hath been brought to nought in his sight” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is perfection, that the malicious one have no force against a man; and that this be “in his sight;” that is, that he know most surely that the malicious is not, save when the mind turns itself away from the eternal and immutable form of her own Creator to the form of the creature, which was made out of nothing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But those that fear the Lord, He glorifieth:” the Lord Himself, that is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now “the fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As then the things above belong to the perfect, so what he is now going to say belongs to beginners.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who sweareth unto his neighbour, and deceiveth him not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who hath not given his money upon usury, and hath not taken rewards against the innocent” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are no great things: but he who is not able to do even this, much less able is he to speak the truth in his heart, and to practise no deceit in his tongue, but as the truth is in the heart, so to profess and have it in his mouth, “yea, yea; nay, nay;” and to do no evil to his neighbour, that is, to any man; and to entertain no slander against his neighbour: all which are the virtues of the perfect, in whose sight the malicious one hath been brought to nought.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet he concludes even these lesser things thus, “Whoso doeth these things shall not be moved for ever:” that is, he shall attain unto those greater things, wherein is great and unshaken stability.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even the very tenses are, perhaps not without cause, so varied, as that in the conclusion above the past tense should be used, but in this the future.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there it was said, “The malicious one hath been brought to nought in his sight:” but here, “shall not be moved for ever.”
